data.title: Altcoin ETF Race Heats Up, Filings Pile Up Despite Choppy Flows
data.description:
Numerous altcoins join the ETF rally, including Canary's uncanny bid for a presidential coin-based ETF product.
source: https://dailycoin.com/altcoin-etf-race-heats-up-filings-pile-up/?utm_source=cryptopanic&utm_medium=rss

data.title: Blackstone Nears $2.5 Billion Acquisition of Champions Group
data.description: Blackstone is reportedly in advanced discussions to purchase the residential services provider Champions Group for approximately $2.5 billion. Bloomberg posted on X, citing sources familiar with the situation, that the negotiations are progressing, although no final agreement has been reached yet. The acquisition, if finalized, would mark a significant expansion for Blackstone in the residential services sector. Further details about the potential deal remain undisclosed at this time.

data.title: A newly created address transferred 25 million WLFI tokens, worth $2.52 million, from Binance.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created address withdrew 25 million WLFI from Binance, worth approximately $2.52 million.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Iran's Supreme Leader Defends Nuclear Rights Amid International Tensions
data.description: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has asserted that nuclear energy is an inalienable right for the nation, emphasizing its inclusion in the guidelines of the International Atomic Energy Agency. According to Jin10, Khamenei stated that all countries possess the right to have nuclear facilities and uranium enrichment capabilities, framing it as a sovereign right. He questioned the United States' interference in Iran's nuclear affairs, highlighting ongoing geopolitical tensions surrounding the issue.

data.title: US-listed company DDC increased its holdings by 80 bitcoins, bringing its total holdings to over 2,000 bitcoins.
data.description: DDC Enterprise Limited (DDC), a US-listed company, announced today that it has increased its holdings of Bitcoin by 80, bringing its total treasury holdings to 2,068 BTC, with an average holding price of $84,944. This latest purchase marks DDC's sixth consecutive week of increasing its Bitcoin holdings, representing a 74.8% increase since the beginning of 2026 and solidifying Bitcoin's position as a fundamental component of the company's financial strategy. The transaction was reportedly completed within DDC's established capital deployment framework, which emphasizes robust scaling, liquidity management, and balance sheet strength. The company's strategy aims to address market cyclical fluctuations while enhancing long-term per-share value. (Businesswire)

data.title: YouTube Star Salish Matter Expands to Television
data.description: Salish Matter, known for her success on YouTube and as a Sephora influencer, is set to make her television debut. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting her journey from online platforms to mainstream media. Matter has gained significant popularity through her engaging content and collaborations, which have resonated with a wide audience. Her transition to television marks a new chapter in her career, expanding her reach beyond digital platforms. This move is expected to further solidify her presence in the entertainment industry, showcasing her versatility and appeal to a broader audience.

data.title: Michael Saylor's Strategy Acquires 2,486 Bitcoin in Major Purchase
data.description: Crypto Rover posted on X that Michael Saylor's company, Strategy, has made a significant acquisition of 2,486 Bitcoin, valued at $168.4 million. This purchase highlights the company's ongoing commitment to investing in cryptocurrency. Saylor, a prominent advocate for Bitcoin, continues to lead his firm in expanding its digital asset holdings. The acquisition reflects a strategic move to strengthen their position in the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Congressman Calls for Review of Overseas Support for Autonomous Vehicles
data.description:
The request marks a shift from Capitol Hill questioning to formal regulatory pressure, as lawmakers raise concerns about safety, accountability, and national security.
source: https://decrypt.co/358267/congressman-overseas-support-autonomous-vehicles

data.title: An Italian banking giant disclosed that it held approximately $96 million worth of Bitcoin spot ETFs by the end of 2025 and had hedged this holding with Strategy put options.
data.description: Italian banking giant Intesa Sanpaolo disclosed in its 13F filing as of December 2025 that it holds approximately $96 million in Bitcoin spot ETFs, including the ARK 21Shares Bitcoin ETF and the iShares Bitcoin Trust. Simultaneously, Intesa Sanpaolo also holds a large put option position against Strategy, which the market interprets as a hedge against the perceived overvaluation of Strategy's stock relative to its Bitcoin asset value (mNAV) while simultaneously holding long Bitcoin ETFs. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Strategy bought another 2,486 BTC, increasing its total holdings to 717,131 BTC.
data.description: Michael Saylor, founder and chairman of Strategy, announced on the X platform that Strategy purchased 2,486 BTC for approximately $168.4 million, at an average price of approximately $67,710. As of February 16, 2026, the company will hold a total of 717,100 BTC, with a total value of approximately $54.52 billion, at an average price of approximately $76,027.

data.title: Phishing Campaign Targets Trezor and Ledger Users with Fake Letters
data.description: A sophisticated phishing campaign is targeting users of hardware wallets Trezor and Ledger through physical mail, including fake letters adorned with holograms and forged signatures. According to NS3.AI, attackers are leveraging leaked personal data from previous breaches to enhance credibility and persuade victims to scan malicious QR codes designed to steal digital assets. Security experts caution that this approach heightens psychological pressure on users and advise them to verify all communications and refrain from sharing wallet backups.

data.title: Centrifuge partners with Pharos to advance on-chain distribution infrastructure for institutional assets.
data.description: According to official news, Centrifuge and Pharos have announced a collaboration aimed at enabling large-scale on-chain distribution and operation of institutional-grade assets, such as tokenized US Treasury bonds (JTRSY) and AAA-rated structured credit products (JAAA), through a shared infrastructure framework. This collaboration will combine Centrifuge's tokenization infrastructure with Pharos' Layer 1 to provide liquidity and distribution layers for Centrifuge-issued assets, addressing the challenges of distributing institutional assets in on-chain finance. Centrifuge Labs CEO Bhaji Illuminati stated that tokenization alone cannot solve accessibility and usability issues; this collaboration focuses on building distribution and infrastructure layers. Pharos CEO Wish Wu stated that the challenge lies not in demand, but in infrastructure, and the collaboration aims to create an environment where institutional assets can remain active on-chain. Pharos is a financial Layer 1 designed for RealFi, developed by a team of former Ant Group leaders and engineers, and has received investment from institutions such as Hack VC and Faction VC.

data.title: AI Demand Drives DRAM Price Increase Amid Supply Constraints
data.description: The rising demand for AI-related applications is causing a significant impact on the DRAM market, leading to tighter supply and increased prices within the chip industry. Bloomberg posted on X that consumer-facing companies are facing challenges as they are compelled to either limit supply or increase prices due to escalating costs. Analyst @rwang07 from SemiAnalysis shared insights on the Odd Lots podcast with hosts @thestalwart and @tracyalloway, discussing the implications for consumers.

data.title: Corporate Efficiency and Its Impact on Jeep Manufacturer
data.description: The pursuit of cost-cutting measures in corporate efficiency can sometimes be excessive, as evidenced by the company responsible for manufacturing Jeeps. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges faced by the automaker in balancing efficiency with operational effectiveness. The focus on reducing expenses has raised concerns about the potential impact on product quality and overall company performance. This situation underscores the delicate balance companies must maintain between cost management and sustaining their core business operations.

data.title: Sanofi and Teva's Experimental Drug Shows Promise for Inflammatory Bowel Disease
data.description: Sanofi announced that an experimental drug developed in collaboration with Teva has shown positive results for patients suffering from inflammatory bowel disease. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential impact of this development on the treatment landscape for the condition. The drug, still in the experimental phase, aims to address the symptoms and improve the quality of life for those affected by the disease. Sanofi and Teva are optimistic about the future of this treatment and are continuing their research efforts to bring it to market. The collaboration between the two pharmaceutical companies underscores their commitment to advancing medical solutions for chronic conditions.

data.title: Citigroup's Profitability Boosts Stock Potential, Analysts Say
data.description: Citigroup's shares are poised for further growth, according to Morgan Stanley analysts, following the stock reaching a 17-year high this month. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the bank's improving profitability as a key factor driving this positive outlook. The analysts suggest that Citigroup's financial performance could continue to enhance its stock value, offering potential for investors.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Trump's International Engagements to Impact U.S. Budget
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's overseas activities are projected to significantly affect the national budget, with expenses potentially reaching billions. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the financial implications of these international endeavors. The costs are attributed to various diplomatic and security measures required during these trips. As the administration continues to engage globally, the financial burden on taxpayers is expected to increase. This development raises questions about the allocation of resources and the long-term economic impact on the United States.

data.title: Mesh Optical Technologies Secures $50 Million to Expand U.S. Manufacturing
data.description: Mesh Optical Technologies, a startup focused on developing high-speed data transfer hardware for data centers, has successfully raised $50 million in a funding round. Bloomberg posted on X that the investment was led by Thrive Capital, founded by Joshua Kushner. The funds will be used to enhance the manufacturing capabilities of the technology within the United States. This move is expected to bolster the company's production capacity and support its growth in the competitive data center market.

data.title: Fertiglobe Eyes Expansion in European Fertilizer Market
data.description: Abu Dhabi-based Fertiglobe, a leading global exporter of nitrogen fertilizers, is exploring opportunities to expand its presence in Europe. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the company is positioning itself to capitalize on the shifting market dynamics caused by restrictions on Russian supplies and the European Union's green initiatives. Fertiglobe's strategic move comes as the EU's policies and supply chain adjustments create new avenues for growth in the fertilizer sector. The company aims to leverage these changes to strengthen its foothold in the European market.

data.title: El Salvador Expands Bitcoin Holdings to 7,565.37 BTC
data.description: El Salvador has bolstered its Bitcoin reserves, now totaling 7,565.37 BTC. According to NS3.AI, this development underscores the nation's continued dedication to its Bitcoin investment strategy. The announcement was made by Cointelegraph on the X social media platform.
